title: Snooze with wake semantics
description: Add `bober task snooze <id> --until <when>` which sets status=snoozed and records a 'snooze-until:<ISO>' tag on the Finding. Update the default list so a snoozed task is hidden while its wake time is in the future (relative to the injected now) and reappears once the wake time has passed.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with the snooze subcommand and the wake-aware list filter added.
A unit test snoozes a task until a fixed future ISO date and asserts the active Finding has status='snoozed' and a tag exactly equal to 'snooze-until:<that ISO>'.
A unit test lists with an injected now BEFORE the wake time and asserts the snoozed task is absent, then lists with an injected now AFTER the wake time and asserts the same task is present again.
A unit test marks a snoozed task done and asserts the transition succeeds and the task leaves the default list.
A unit test passes an unparseable --until value and asserts the handler reports the error, sets exitCode=1, and does not throw.

nonGoals: Do NOT add a new schema field to the Finding for the wake time — reuse the existing tags[] with a 'snooze-until:<ISO>' entry.
Do NOT auto-wake tasks via a background timer — waking is computed lazily at list time against the injected now.
Do NOT alter the done/drop/start transitions from sprint 2 beyond letting them clear a snoozed task.
Do NOT read the clock inside the list filter — the comparison now is injected.
stopConditions: `npm run build` exits 0 and the snooze + wake-filter unit tests pass.
`bober task snooze <id> --until <future>` then `bober task list` omits the task, and listing again after the wake time shows it.
definitionOfDone: A user can defer a task with `bober task snooze <id> --until <when>`; the task gets status=snoozed plus a 'snooze-until:<ISO>' tag, disappears from the default `bober task list` until its wake time passes (compared against an injected now), then returns to the list. A snoozed task can still be completed or dropped, and an invalid --until is reported on stderr with exitCode=1 without throwing.
assumptions: Wake time is parsed from --until into an ISO string at the handler boundary and stored as the tag 'snooze-until:<ISO>'; the list filter parses it back and compares to the injected now.
A snoozed task whose wake time has passed is treated as visible (status displayed as snoozed-due/woken) so the user re-triages it.
outOfScope: Reminders/notifications when a task wakes (telegram/scheduler scope).
generatorNotes: Add a `snooze <id>` subcommand to src/cli/commands/task.ts with a required option --until <when>; parse <when> to an ISO string at the boundary (accept an ISO date/datetime; on parse failure, chalk.red + exitCode=1 + return). Reuse transitionFinding from sprint 2 with newStatus='snoozed' and a mutate that appends 'snooze-until:'+iso to tags (strip any prior snooze-until tag first so re-snoozing replaces it). Extend the default list filter in finding-store.ts/task.ts: a finding is visible when status in {open,in-progress} OR (status='snoozed' AND its snooze-until tag parses to a time <= now). Keep now injectable so tests can pass a fixed clock. Do not change snooze for already-terminal tasks.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm no new field was added to the Finding schema — the wake time must live in tags[] as 'snooze-until:<ISO>'. Verify the list filter takes now as a parameter and that the before/after-wake tests flip visibility purely from the injected now (no real clock). Confirm re-snoozing replaces rather than stacks the snooze-until tag. Verify done on a snoozed task still works.
